pattern: A running prefix sum of letter values lets you test every split point for an equal-halves balance in a single pass.
hints: Translate each letter to its alphabet position and think about the total of the whole string.
A split is balanced exactly when the left part's total is half of the whole string's total.
If the total is odd no split works; otherwise sweep a prefix sum and return true the moment it reaches half the total before the last character.

edge_inputs.description: balanced split exists in the middle
odd total can never split evenly
split is not at the midpoint of length

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: constructed balanced strings to exercise the true branch
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    half = max(1, min(size, 49))
    left = [rng.randint(1, 26) for _ in range(half)]
    target = sum(left)
    right = []
    rem = target
    while rem > 0:
        v = min(rem, rng.randint(1, 26))
        right.append(v)
        rem -= v
    chars = [chr(ord('a') + v - 1) for v in left + right]
    return [''.join(chars)]
stress.note: left and right both sum to target so a balanced split exists
